title: The Christ Nail

jokes.text: Boy, they sure make a lot of noise.
jokes.setup: Larry's new orthotics are introduced as helping his feet
jokes.punchline: The immediate complaint about their loudness sets up the episode's running gag
jokes.explanation: Efficient setup for the episode's main running gag. Classic Larry observation that will pay off throughout.

jokes.text: so every anti-semite in the neighborhood will know that we live here in case they wanna burn down the house
jokes.setup: Larry explains the mezuzah as a Jewish religious item
jokes.punchline: Subverts expectation by suggesting it advertises to anti-semites rather than protects
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Larry humor - takes religious tradition and finds the darkest possible interpretation. Character-specific and subversive.

jokes.text: "How do you feel, Mr. David?" That's good. That's good, Jesús.
jokes.setup: Larry teaches Jesus to speak loudly to his deaf father
jokes.punchline: The absurd formality of practicing the exact phrase creates awkward comedy
jokes.explanation: Mild character comedy showing Larry's controlling nature and cultural awkwardness.

jokes.text: By the way, Jesús... Jesus... either one? Either one, si. It's okay. Same thing. Si.
jokes.setup: Larry uncertain about pronunciation of Jesus's name
jokes.punchline: Jesus's casual acceptance that his name is literally Jesus, same as Christ
jokes.explanation: Sets up the religious irony that will pay off later. Larry's typical awkwardness around cultural differences.

jokes.text: Do you want your life changed, Jesus?
jokes.setup: Jesus mentions foot pain
jokes.punchline: Larry dramatically oversells orthotics as life-changing to someone literally named Jesus
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony of Larry offering to change the life of someone named Jesus. The religious implications make it funnier.

jokes.text: De naaadaaa.
jokes.setup: Larry says 'de nada' normally
jokes.punchline: Extends it into an exaggerated, theatrical pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry showing off, overplaying his limited Spanish knowledge.

jokes.text: Well, they're very good for my feet, I could tell you that. My feet feel a lot better.
jokes.setup: Cheryl complains about the loud orthotics
jokes.punchline: Larry dismisses her valid complaint by focusing on his own benefit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry selfishness - completely missing the point of others' complaints.

jokes.text: Why is it that before you have a meal, you do the whole cross thing, but not for snack. I don't think it applies to snacks.
jokes.setup: Larry observes religious crossing before meals
jokes.punchline: Applies literal logic to spiritual practice, wondering about snack exemptions
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry taking religious practice to its logical extreme. Sharp observational comedy.

jokes.text: There's gotta be a maximum number of times somebody can cross in a day. Uh, I wonder what the record is.
jokes.setup: Discussion of crossing frequency
jokes.punchline: Escalates to wondering about crossing records like it's a sport
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that treats religious observance like competitive athletics. Very Larry.

jokes.text: This is a nail. I bought this on the internet. It's from 'The Passion of the Christ'.
jokes.setup: Character shows a nail
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's supposedly from the crucifixion movie - absurd movie memorabilia
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the episode's climax. Absurd premise that movie props are religious relics.

jokes.text: I can see worshipping Jesus if he were a girl. Like if god had a daughter... Jane. I'll worship a Jane.
jokes.setup: Discussion of worshipping Jesus
jokes.punchline: Larry suggests he'd only worship a female deity named Jane
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly subversive religious commentary. The name 'Jane' makes it even funnier - so mundane for the daughter of God.

jokes.text: But, you know, to worship a guy... it's like a little kinda, you know, it's a little gay, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Continuing discussion of worshipping Jesus
jokes.punchline: Larry suggests male worship has homosexual implications
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry inappropriate observation. Less sophisticated than the Jane joke but still character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Good-looking woman, zaftig, you know? Good sense of humor.
jokes.setup: Larry's fantasy about God's daughter Jane
jokes.punchline: Describes his ideal deity with specific physical preferences
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that makes the religious fantasy even more absurd by getting specific about physical attributes.

jokes.text: I was... your bra was kind of... it was about to fall off and I was... I was putting it back on.
jokes.setup: Susie catches Larry doing something suspicious
jokes.punchline: Larry's ridiculous excuse for looking at her bra
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment. Larry's transparent lie makes it worse, classic awkward comedy.

jokes.text: I didn't deliberately set out to look at the size, but I was... I had the bra in my hand, and it's kind of like, you know, if you have a driver's license you check out the date of birth.
jokes.setup: Larry tries to justify looking at bra size
jokes.punchline: Compares it to automatically checking birth date on license - absurd comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliant analogy that makes Larry's creepiness seem like innocent curiosity. Classic LD logic.

jokes.text: I'm not gonna have that conversation with someone. I would rather just get rid of her.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to tell housekeeper to wear bra
jokes.punchline: Cheryl prefers firing someone rather than having awkward conversation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Cheryl's conflict avoidance taken to extreme.

jokes.text: Oh hi, I thought you were Jesús! Jesús! No kidding. Yeah, his shoes sound like your shoes.
jokes.setup: Larry's loud orthotics
jokes.punchline: Jesus now has identical loud shoes, creating confusion
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the running gag by spreading it to another character.

jokes.text: 'Cause you know, between you and me, he could be quite a downer this guy, this Jesús.
jokes.setup: Discussing Jesus being happier with the orthotics
jokes.punchline: Larry calling someone literally named Jesus a 'downer'
jokes.explanation: Subtle irony - calling Jesus depressing. Larry's obliviousness to the religious implications.

jokes.text: you don't wear a, uh... bra.
jokes.setup: Larry needs to address the bra issue
jokes.punchline: His extreme discomfort saying the word 'bra' to his housekeeper
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Larry's discomfort with the topic he created.

jokes.text: Yeah, a lot of flopping going on.
jokes.setup: Discussing the distracting nature of no bra
jokes.punchline: Larry's crude description makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Peak awkwardness as Larry makes the conversation worse with crude specificity.

jokes.text: They're inviting and cozy and cottony. What do you call that cotton? Puma? That's a sneaker. All right... pima! Pima!
jokes.setup: Larry trying to sell the idea of modern bras
jokes.punchline: Confuses pima cotton with Puma sneakers, then corrects himself
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry malapropism and recovery. Shows his try-hard nature in uncomfortable situations.

jokes.text: I got a housekeeper who steals.
jokes.setup: Jeff can't find his can opener
jokes.punchline: Immediately jumps to conclusion that housekeeper is a thief
jokes.explanation: Efficient character beat showing Jeff's paranoid leap to conclusions.

jokes.text: You had to tell your housekeeper to wear a bra? I am buying her!
jokes.setup: Larry mentions telling housekeeper to wear bra
jokes.punchline: Larry escalates to buying her one, showing how deep he's gotten into this
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how Larry's 'simple' solution has become complicated.

jokes.text: What, are you kidding? She's like 11 years old... She still believes in the tooth fairy?
jokes.setup: Discussion of Sammi's tooth falling out
jokes.punchline: Larry's shocked disbelief that an 11-year-old still believes
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry inappropriate honesty about childhood beliefs. Sets up later payoff.

jokes.text: I knew there was no tooth fairy even before my teeth fell out.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Larry's childhood beliefs
jokes.punchline: Larry was cynical even as a small child
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment that explains everything about Larry in one line. Beautifully crafted.

jokes.text: I think she's about the same size as Maria, my housekeeper.
jokes.setup: Looking at Susie
jokes.punchline: Larry reveals he's been sizing up Susie for bra comparison
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reveals Larry's been plotting this comparison all along.

jokes.text: I just found out, and I gotta tell you something. A little disappointed.
jokes.setup: Jeff asks about Cheryl's bra size
jokes.punchline: Larry admits disappointment in his wife's breast size
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry inappropriateness - disappointed in his own wife's body and sharing it.

jokes.text: You don't tell me Cheryl's, I ain't telling you Susie's.
jokes.setup: Negotiation over bra size information
jokes.punchline: Jeff turns it into a trade negotiation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that turns intimate information into commodity trading.

jokes.text: I'm gonna kill him! It's you! You're the tooth fairy!
jokes.setup: Sammi catches someone putting money under pillow
jokes.punchline: Child's world shattered, Susie threatens Larry
jokes.explanation: Solid payoff to earlier setup. Larry's orthotics cause the childhood-destroying revelation.

jokes.text: I know, but it had to happen eventually.
jokes.setup: Susie furious about ruined tooth fairy
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact response to destroying child's innocence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry response - completely missing emotional impact, just cold logic.

jokes.text: It might be a blessing in disguise. The kid's 11 years old already. Enough with the tooth fairy!
jokes.setup: Larry trying to defend himself
jokes.punchline: Makes it worse by arguing the kid needed disillusionment
jokes.explanation: Larry doubles down instead of apologizing - classic character consistency.

jokes.text: you misanthropic moron! Maybe you shouldn't have propagated this ridiculous myth!
jokes.setup: Susie's rage at Larry
jokes.punchline: Larry's intellectual argument about 'propagating myths' while being thrown out
jokes.explanation: Great escalation where Larry tries to win argument with vocabulary while being ejected.

jokes.text: Not that there's anything wrong with it. But there is something wrong with it. What am I saying? There's a lot wrong with it.
jokes.setup: Saleswoman suggests men wearing women's underwear
jokes.punchline: Larry's contradictory moral stance in real time
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - trying to be PC then abandoning it for honesty mid-sentence.

jokes.text: I'm just saying this kind of foamy, soft material would be nice down there for me. That doesn't make me a pervert just 'cause I want to wear underwear made out of a bra.
jokes.setup: Larry feeling bra material
jokes.punchline: Defends wanting bra-material underwear while insisting he's not a pervert
jokes.explanation: Peak awkwardness as Larry digs deeper into inappropriate territory while protesting innocence.

jokes.text: I got a feeling I think I nailed it. I really do!
jokes.setup: Larry presents bras to Maria without knowing her size
jokes.punchline: Confident he 'nailed it' despite having no real information
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Larry's unfounded confidence. 'Nailed it' gains significance later.

jokes.text: You're a sick fuck, you know that?
jokes.setup: Susie has been watching security footage
jokes.punchline: Reveals Larry's been caught on camera doing something creepy
jokes.explanation: Great escalation reveal that Larry's been caught. Sets up the cringe video evidence.

jokes.text: I was trying to imagine what it would be like to be the housekeeper and have those breasts you know, working and doing dishes.
jokes.setup: Larry tries to explain his behavior on camera
jokes.punchline: His explanation makes it sound even more perverted
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe as Larry's 'innocent' explanation sounds completely perverted.

jokes.text: Because there is no fucking tooth fairy! And It's about time she learned that!
jokes.setup: Susie blaming Larry for ruining Sammi's tooth fairy belief
jokes.punchline: Larry's aggressive defense of childhood disillusionment
jokes.explanation: Larry doubling down on his anti-tooth fairy stance with passionate conviction. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: Oh, Jesús was very upset that you were talking about my breasts and wanting to buy a bra for me, so he said, 'Mr. David can go fuck himself'.
jokes.setup: Larry asking where Jesus is
jokes.punchline: Jesus (literally) told Larry to go fuck himself over the bra situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff - Jesus (the person) rejecting Larry creates perfect religious irony. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: That's blasphemy! He took my nail! He took the nail right off my neck! That's Jesus' nail!
jokes.setup: Larry uses Mel's nail for the mezuzah
jokes.punchline: Mel's horror at his 'sacred' movie prop being used functionally
jokes.explanation: Great escalation where the ridiculous movie prop becomes 'sacred' when taken.

jokes.text: My god, she makes chicken salad, she wears brassieres, she does it all!
jokes.setup: Praising Maria's chicken salad
jokes.punchline: Includes bra-wearing as one of her professional accomplishments
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that treats wearing a bra as a professional skill alongside cooking.

jokes.text: I don't know how I do it. Maybe you can get a job in a carnival somewhere. Yeah, have your own little booth. Step right up. You are... 36c!
jokes.setup: Larry boasting about guessing bra size correctly
jokes.punchline: Jeff suggests Larry become a carnival bra-size guesser
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that imagines Larry's inappropriate skill as a carnival act.

jokes.text: What fuckin' idiot put bones in a sandwich? I bit into a bone! There's bones in the chicken salad!
jokes.setup: Jeff praising Maria's chicken salad earlier
jokes.punchline: Immediately bites into bone and curses the 'perfect' chicken salad
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal that undercuts the earlier praise with immediate reality.

jokes.text: The nail. I must have dropped it! It saved me!
jokes.setup: Jesus stepped on something that caused him to fall and avoid danger
jokes.punchline: Larry realizes the 'Christ nail' actually saved Jesus
jokes.explanation: Brilliant culmination where the absurd movie prop 'Christ nail' literally saves a man named Jesus. Perfect ironic closure.

tmdb_overview: A undergarment purchase for his housekeeper lands Larry in hot water, while his father-in-law's fondness for The Passion of the Christ comes in handy more than once.
